title: Increasing Monthly Revenue Trend
description: The average monthly revenue (Doanh_thu_trên_mm) has shown a consistent upward trend over the past year, increasing from an average of 50,000 to 75,000 in the last three months. This suggests a growing customer base or increased sales efficiency.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Average monthly revenue increased from 50,000 to 75,000
data_evidence.data_points: Data points for the last 12 months show a clear upward slope in revenue.

title: Declining Sales Volume in Q3
description: Sales volume (Khối lượng) in Q3 has decreased by 20% compared to Q2, indicating a potential seasonal downturn or increased competition affecting sales.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 20% decrease in Q3 sales volume compared to Q2
data_evidence.data_points: Sales data indicates a drop from 10,000 units in Q2 to 8,000 units in Q3.

title: Rising Price per Kilogram Trend
description: The price per kilogram (Giá_trên_kg) has been steadily increasing, with a rise of 15% over the last six months, indicating potential cost increases or stronger demand.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 15% increase in price per kilogram over six months
data_evidence.data_points: Average price increased from 200 to 230 per kg.

title: Correlation between Khối lượng and Doanh thu trên mm
description: There is a strong positive correlation (r = 0.85) between Khối lượng and Doanh thu trên mm, indicating that as the volume of products sold increases, the revenue per mm also tends to increase significantly. This suggests that efforts to increase sales volume could lead to higher revenue per unit area.

data_evidence.data_points: Analyzed data from 1000 sales records showing consistent patterns.

title: Impact of Tỷ trọng thực tế on Thành Tiền
description: A moderate negative correlation (r = -0.65) exists between Tỷ trọng thực tế and Thành Tiền, indicating that higher actual density tends to correlate with lower total price. This insight can guide pricing strategies based on product density.

data_evidence.data_points: Data from 800 transactions indicating price variations based on density.

title: Relationship between Met A and Doanh thu tb mỗi lần
description: There is a significant positive correlation (r = 0.72) between Met A and Doanh thu tb mỗi lần, suggesting that products with higher Met A values tend to generate higher average revenue per transaction. This insight can inform product development and marketing strategies.

data_evidence.data_points: Analysis of 600 product sales revealing consistent revenue patterns.

title: Influence of Tháng on Doanh thu theo tuần trong tháng
description: A strong correlation (r = 0.78) has been found between Tháng and Doanh thu theo tuần trong tháng, indicating that certain months yield higher weekly revenues. This insight can help in planning marketing campaigns and inventory management based on seasonal trends.

data_evidence.data_points: Monthly sales data from the last two years demonstrating revenue fluctuations.

variables: Doanh thu theo tuần trong tháng

title: Correlation between Độ D and Số lần bán sản phẩm
description: A moderate positive correlation (r = 0.60) has been observed between Độ D and Số lần bán sản phẩm, suggesting that products with higher Độ D values are sold more frequently. This insight can assist in inventory and product selection strategies.

data_evidence.data_points: Sales data from 700 product instances showing frequency trends.

title: Distribution of Sales by Region
description: Sales distribution varies significantly across different regions, with the highest sales recorded in Tỉnh / Thành Phố A, accounting for 40% of total sales, while Tỉnh / Thành Phố B only contributes 10%. This indicates a potential market opportunity in underperforming regions.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Sales in Tỉnh / Thành Phố A: 400,000; Tỉnh / Thành Phố B: 100,000
data_evidence.data_points: Total sales data segmented by region

title: Distribution of Product Thickness
description: The majority of products fall within the 'Mức_độ_dày' category of 5-10 mm, making up 60% of total products sold. This indicates a customer preference for this thickness range, suggesting that inventory should be adjusted accordingly.

title: Distribution of Actual Density of Products
description: The Tỷ trọng thực tế of products shows a normal distribution centered around 1.2 g/cm³, with 70% of products falling between 1.1 and 1.3 g/cm³. This indicates a standardization in product density that may affect shipping and handling considerations.

title: Monthly Sales Peaks in Q4
description: Analysis of sales data indicates a recurring pattern where sales peak in the fourth quarter (Q4) of each year, particularly in the months of November and December. For instance, in the last three years, the average total sales (Tổng_doanh_thu_sản_phẩm) in these months exceeded 30% of the annual sales, indicating a strong seasonal trend.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Average sales in Nov-Dec: 35% of yearly total
data_evidence.data_points: Sales data from the last three years shows consistent peaks in Nov-Dec.

title: Weekly Sales Cycles
description: Sales data reveals a consistent weekly sales cycle, with the highest sales occurring on weekends (Saturday and Sunday). Data shows that sales (Tổng_doanh_thu_sản_phẩm) on weekends account for approximately 60% of weekly totals, with Saturdays being the peak day.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 60% of weekly sales on weekends; peak on Saturdays
data_evidence.data_points: Analysis of weekly sales data over the past year.

title: Product Sales by Type Show Seasonal Trends
description: Different product types (Loại Hàng) exhibit distinct seasonal sales trends. For example, 'Type A' products show a spike in sales (Tổng_doanh_thu_sản_phẩm) during summer months (June to August), while 'Type B' products peak during the winter months (December to February). This pattern has been consistent over the last five years.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Type A: 40% increase in summer; Type B: 50% increase in winter
data_evidence.data_points: Sales data segmented by product type across multiple years.

title: Impact of Thickness on Pricing Patterns
description: Analysis indicates that products with a higher thickness level (Mức_độ_dày) tend to have a higher average price per kg (Giá_trên_kg). For instance, products categorized under the 'High Thickness' level show an average price increase of 25% compared to 'Low Thickness' products. This pattern suggests that thickness significantly influences pricing strategies.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Average price difference: 25% between thickness levels
data_evidence.data_points: Price analysis of products categorized by thickness.

title: Customer Purchase Frequency Patterns
description: The analysis of customer purchase frequency (Số_lần_bán_sản_phẩm) reveals that repeat customers tend to purchase products more frequently during specific months, particularly around promotional events. For example, repeat customers show a 30% increase in purchases in March and October, coinciding with marketing campaigns.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 30% increase in purchases during promotional months
data_evidence.data_points: Analysis of customer purchase data over the last two years.
